filament-flow.title: Filament Flow
filament-flow.description: Business Process Management plugin that enables workflow and state machine capabilities within Filament.
A descriptive name to identify this work: A descriptive name to identify this workflow (e.g., "Order Processing", "Invoice Approval").
The Eloquent model this workflow will be: The Eloquent model this workflow will be attached to.
The varchar column on the model table th: The varchar column on the model table that stores the current workflow state.
Optional key-value pairs for storing add: Optional key-value pairs for storing additional workflow configuration.
Configure visibility and mutability for:
Configure visibility and mutability for each component (form fields, sections, tabs, table columns) in this state.

**Mutability levels**: Editable — full access; Read-only — visible but not modifiable; Locked — completely hidden and blocked.

You can add **role overrides** to each field to grant different permissions per role. When no override matches the user's role, the base field setting applies.
Define the basic settings for this workf: Define the basic settings for this workflow. Choose a name, select the Eloquent model it applies to, and specify the database column that tracks the current state. Creation permissions and field visibility are configured on each state via the States relation — the initial state controls who can create records and what they see in the creation form.
Enter the Filament component name (secti: Enter the Filament component name (section, tab, or custom field) that does not correspond to a database column.
Cannot delete state with existing transi: Cannot delete state with existing transitions. Remove transitions first.
The key is the internal identifier used: The key is the internal identifier used in code and transitions. The label is the human-readable name shown in the UI.
Unique identifier for this state. Use sn: Unique identifier for this state. Use snake_case (e.g., pending, in_review, approved).
Display name shown to users in badges, s: Display name shown to users in badges, selects, and notifications.
Optional note to describe the purpose of: Optional note to describe the purpose of this state.
Customize how this state is displayed in: Customize how this state is displayed in the interface.
Optional icon displayed alongside the st: Optional icon displayed alongside the state label.
Determines the display order among state: Determines the display order among states.
Define the role of this state within the: Define the role of this state within the workflow lifecycle.
Records start in this state when created: Records start in this state when created.
No further transitions are allowed from: No further transitions are allowed from this state.
Optional state class for custom logic (e: Optional state class for custom logic (e.g., validation, side effects).
Configure the basic properties of this s: Configure the basic properties of this state.
Whether this field is shown or hidden in: Whether this field is shown or hidden in this state.
Editable — full access; Read-only — visi: Editable — full access; Read-only — visible but not modifiable; Locked — completely hidden and blocked.
Make this field mandatory in this state.
Determines the display order among field: Determines the display order among fields.
Additional Laravel validation rules appl: Additional Laravel validation rules applied in this state.
